content: Measuring waist circumference is recommended during times of weight loss or muscle gain as a proxy for body fat.
Waist circumference is impacted by abdominal fat, undigested food, transit time, fiber intake, and water retention.
To measure waist circumference accurately, it should be taken at a consistent time since the last meal.
Transitioning to a higher fiber diet can temporarily increase waist circumference due to slower transit time.
Waist circumference may not reflect weight loss if hormonal factors influence water retention, such as during a woman's menstrual cycle.
Waist circumference can be a useful metric if it declines logically with weight loss and does not show erratic behavior.
